commands.repair_reason: The remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real Telegram proof and maintainer review of whether to require the focused regression test; ClawSweeper should not replace that proof with an automated repair.; Cleared: The diff only widens the Telegram HTML allowlist to a literal boolean expandable attribute and adds no dependency, workflow, secret, or executable supply-chain change. (sha=4fa6b9896ff9a14297cbc8cc7faeb5056b0720e8)
commands.review_summary:
The branch moves `blockquote` from the simple Telegram HTML tag set to the attribute-pattern map so plain `<blockquote>` and `<blockquote expandable>` pass sanitization.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main rejects `<blockquote expandable>` because `blockquote` is treated as an attribute-free simple tag, so the reported sanitizer behavior is reproducible from the formatter path even without running tests.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real Telegram proof and maintainer review of whether to require the focused regression test; ClawSweeper should not replace that proof with an automated repair.

target.title: fix(telegram): allow expandable blockquotes
target.body:
## Summary - Problem: `
` is stripped in Telegram HTML output. `blockquote` lives in `TELEGRAM_SIMPLE_HTML_TAGS`, which only permits the tag with zero attributes, so the `expandable` attribute fails sanitization. - Why it matters: Telegram natively supports expandable blockquotes; agents can't emit them today. - What changed: Move `blockquote` from the simple-tag set to `TELEGRAM_ATTR_HTML_TAG_PATTERNS` with pattern `/^(\s+expandable)?\s*$/`, which accepts both `
` and `
`. - What did NOT change: All other tags; plain `
` and `
` still pass. ## Change Type - [x] Bug fix ## Real behavior proof - Behavior addressed: expandable blockquotes now survive Telegram HTML sanitization. - Real environment tested: OpenClaw Telegram channel, agent reply containing `
...
`. - Before: the `expandable` attribute was dropped, message rendered as a normal (non-collapsible) blockquote. - After: message renders as a collapsible/expandable blockquote in the Telegram client. - Not tested: nested blockquotes (unchanged behavior). ## Note AI-assisted, single-line change.
target.author: jzakirov
